scriptId: cs_v1_EARLY_L40_Relationship with a boss or coworker
historyId: h_v1_EARLY_L40_Relationship with a boss or coworker

goal: Improve visibility and attribution for contributed work in public updates.
constraints: Do not schedule meetings on Fridays due to recurring personal commitment.
I limit synchronous check-ins to a strict maximum of 25 minutes; I won't do longer blocks.
When I follow up in writing, our archive requires the summary to be exactly three bullet points.
open: Checklist before escalation:\n- Confirm attempts and context: verify you’ve tried available fixes, reviewed relevant docs/tickets, and can briefly explain what was tried and why it didn’t resolve the problem.\\n- Gather concise evidence: assemble a short timeline, the specific impact, affected users/systems, and any key logs or screenshots — stick to facts.\\n- Define the request and urgency: state the exact decision or support you need, why escalation is necessary, and the desired timeframe.\\n- Propose recommended next steps: offer one or two practical options or mitigations and note any constraints or dependencies.\\n- Share a concise summary for the record: include a clear 3‑bullet recap (impact, request, next step) when you escalate and offer a brief sync only if needed.
Open item: handle_visibility_issue for peer contributions; align attribution in future public updates.
Open item: draft messaging to request updated deadline estimates when a teammate repeatedly misses commitments.
Open item: provide short scripts for politely declining unpaid side work pitched by coworkers.
Open item: draft standard brief paragraph to request more context for tickets with vague details.
